adds.rationale: Fills the rockets/spacecraft gap named in the profile thesis; SpaceX's two 2023 Starship test flights and a growing commercial-launch backlog mark this wave's buildup phase.

news_evidence.summary: SpaceX's first full Starship integrated test flight on April 20, 2023; industry observers framed the flight as a milestone for ultra-low-cost launch economics.
Industry analysis on Starship's potential to transform the launch market via dramatic per-kg cost reductions and an early commercial backlog.
Trade press piece arguing the broader space economy is positioning around Starship-class capacity becoming available later in the decade.

adds.rationale: Broadens robotics exposure beyond BOTZ and the single-stock TSLA bet; ARKQ adds autonomous-vehicle, drone, and 3D-printing names that map to the robotics next-wave bucket the profile calls out.

news_evidence.summary: ARKQ ended 2023 up ~40.7%, well above the autonomy/robotics category average of ~21.4%, signaling investor flows into the autonomy/robotics theme.
Industry coverage of robotics moving past trade-show demos into real deployments in manufacturing, logistics, and fulfillment as labor constraints bite.

removes.rationale: AI bucket is over-stacked (5 of 12); among the megacaps AAPL has the weakest articulated AI commercial strategy at year-end 2023, with no announced flagship AI product while MSFT (Copilot/OpenAI), GOOGL (Gemini Dec 6, 2023), and NVDA (data-center GPU surge) each carry clearer AI revenue stories.

news_evidence.summary: Nvidia's data-center revenue surge through 2023 highlighted that AI infrastructure spend was flowing to GPU/cloud incumbents, not to AAPL's device franchise.
Google publicly launched its Gemini multimodal model in December 2023, sharpening the megacap AI race and underscoring that AAPL had not yet entered the consumer-AI fray.

removes.rationale: Defense primes are not part of the profile's named wave thesis (AI, robotics, rockets, nuclear, quantum, engineered biology); SPY already covers diversified large-cap exposure and the watchlist slot is better used on a thesis-aligned ticker.

news_evidence.summary: Lockheed Martin reported Q3 2023 results showing flat-to-down sales growth and ongoing F-35 delivery delays, with the thesis driven more by sustained geopolitical demand than by any technology-wave inflection.

rationale_overall: Net-zero on watchlist size (12 in, 12 out) but a thematic re-shape: trim the over-stacked AI megacap bucket (drop AAPL, the laggard on AI commercial milestones at year-end 2023) and drop LMT (defense, off-thesis), redeploying both slots into next-wave buckets the profile explicitly names but the current watchlist underweights - rockets/spacecraft via ARKX and broader robotics/autonomy via ARKQ.
